name: Credit Helpers
description_short: Credit Helpers disputes errors and inaccurate items on credit reports to help consumers improve their credit scores through a performance-based service model.
description_long:
Credit Helpers, LLC is a credit repair company founded by professionals with several decades of experience in financial services. The founders created the company after noticing a gap in the market for genuinely consumer-friendly credit repair solutions. Their mission centers on addressing the widespread problem of credit reporting errors—studies cited on their website indicate approximately 77% of consumers have inaccuracies on their credit reports that can negatively impact major financial decisions.

The company offers three primary services: credit repair through disputing incorrect items on credit reports, credit building assistance, and financial education. They provide a free credit review and consultation with no obligation, during which customers receive a walkthrough of their credit reports from major bureaus and a summary of how Credit Helpers can assist. Customers gain access to an online portal to monitor progress, ongoing communication from dedicated account managers, and educational resources throughout their engagement.

Credit Helpers distinguishes itself through a performance-based pricing model where the company's success is tied directly to customer success. They emphasize a "pro-consumer" approach, describing their service as straightforward and simple rather than complex or intimidating. Customer testimonials highlight professionalism, courtesy, efficiency, and lack of aggressive sales tactics. One customer reported improving their credit score to the 700s range, though the company notes that score results can vary and 700+ scores are not guaranteed.

While the company presents compelling customer testimonials and a reasonable service model, consumers should note that credit repair results are variable and dependent on individual circumstances. The website does not provide transparent pricing information, specific timelines for results, or detailed explanation of their dispute process. Potential customers should clarify costs and realistic expectations during the free consultation before committing.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. Credit Helpers does not guarantee specific score improvements or the removal of any particular item from your credit reports.

tiers.name: Basic
features: Credit report analysis and error identification

tiers.name: Standard
features: All three bureau disputes per cycle
Cease and desist letters to collectors

pros: Offers completely free initial credit review and consultation with no obligation
Performance-based pricing model ties company success to customer results
Provides online customer portal for real-time progress monitoring
Dedicated account managers offer ongoing communication and support
Includes financial education resources as part of service
Multiple customer testimonials from different states confirming score improvements
Explicitly emphasizes avoiding aggressive or pushy sales tactics
cons: Website does not disclose specific pricing or service fees upfront
No clear timeline provided for how long credit repair disputes typically take
Limited details on the actual dispute methodology or process used
Company disclaimer states 700+ credit scores are 'not a guarantee,' indicating variable results
No information about licensing, certifications, or regulatory compliance details
best_for: Consumers with confirmed errors on their credit reports seeking professional dispute assistance
People overwhelmed by the credit repair process who prefer guided, professional support
Individuals preparing for major purchases (home, auto) and needing credit improvement before applying
diagnosis: Credit Helpers is best for consumers with documented errors on their credit reports who want professional assistance with disputes and prefer a guided approach over DIY methods. The main caveat is that pricing is not transparent upfront, credit score improvements are not guaranteed, and timelines for results are not clearly specified—all details that should be clarified during the free consultation.
services: Credit dispute services for incorrect credit report items
Online customer portal for progress monitoring
Credit report walkthrough with major bureaus
Personalized analysis of individual financial situations
typical_results_timeline: Initial dispute responses typically arrive within 30-45 days of submission. Meaningful score improvements generally occur within 3-6 months, with complex cases involving multiple negative items potentially requiring 6-12 months of active service.
